title: default_mainnet_endpoints_are_https has weaker length assertion than adjacent test
description: In client.rs lines 358-374, the test `default_mainnet_endpoints_are_https` asserts `DEFAULT_MAINNET_ENDPOINTS.len() >= 2`, but the adjacent test `default_mainnet_endpoints_len_at_least_three` (lines 346-352) asserts `>= 3`. If the endpoint list shrinks from 3 to 2 entries, the https-scheme test would still pass. The weaker assertion is redundant and misleading: its doc comment says 'at least two entries' but the spec (agoraold-060.1) and adjacent test require three. Fix: either raise the assertion in this test to >= 3 (matching the other test), or remove the length check from this test entirely since it is already covered.
close_reason: P3/P4 deferred: below stopping threshold (P0=0, P1=0, SEC7 says STOP)
